channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Cafe, 1st July
item.description:
Croydon Road recreation ground. When is cafe opening?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Croydon Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Commercial bins overflowing with fly tipping, 1st July
item.description:
This location has become a recurring fly tipping point in the area. It needs a proper solution that is sustainable as this encourages bad behaviour. This entire junction needs a redesign with trees and landscaping. Please address urgently!!!!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Highbury New Park, Highbury, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 127 Weedington Road NW5 4NX in garden, 1st July
item.description:
The tree is very large and extremely leafy which is touching my window. When it is windy, it bangs on my window. A squirrel came in the window 2 weeks ago and I had a net on it but it broke through it and ran around the house. It is getting worse every year. I live at 127 Weedington Road NW5 4NX and the tree is in the garden at the back, not street side.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 156-158, Weedington Road, Gospel Oak, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Forest bike dumped here for about a month or more, 1st July
item.description:
Forest bike dumped

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mitchison Road, Islington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Long Street, Hackney, London

item.title: Graffiti on shopfront, 1st July
item.description:
Graffiti on shopfront shutters at 135 Homerton High Street E9. Please arrange removal.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Homerton High Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dyne rd outside the council flats, 1st July
item.description:
There is constant daily dumping of waste at this location, including overflowing bins, household rubbish and large items of furniture. It has effectively become treated as a public dumping ground. The council needs to implement a proper long-term solution rather than repeatedly clearing the area as a temporary fix. The current approach is not preventing the problem and is likely to become increasingly costly due to the ongoing use of contracted waste removal services. Please investigate the cause of the repeated dumping and consider measures such as improved bin capacity, more frequent collections, CCTV, signage or enforcement action.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Dyne Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Large - More than a van load (Estates)

item.title: Illegal parking by mechanics preventing rubbish collection, 1st July
item.description:
This is a continuous issue and has prevented refuse from number 14 being collected on many occasions this year. It's something the council should be alerted to and take action on, because it's also a safety issue. Prams and buggies can often not exit too.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Andre Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet
